g.text: · Checking remote datasets...
· Found 7 remote datasets.
· Inspecting dataset econ...
· Inspected remote dataset econ.
Short answer: use ACS + market price/rent series.
Best baseline
- US Census ACS 5-year: household income, gross rent, tenure, bedrooms (for cost-burden and HAI-style metrics). Use PUMS
for micro or summary tables for tract/county.
- HUD CHAS: precomputed cost-burden distributions by income bins at tract/city/county.
- HUD FMR + Income Limits: anchors voucher affordability and policy thresholds.
Market dynamics (pair with income)
- Zillow: ZORI (rent), ZHVI (home values), inventory; metro/county/ZIP monthly.
- Redfin Data Center: median sale price, price per sqft, inventory, days-on-market; weekly/monthly, metro/county/ZIP.
- Freddie Mac PMMS: mortgage rates (for ownership affordability).
- BLS CPI shelter: context for real vs nominal.
If outside the US
- OECD Housing Affordability, Eurostat SILC (income + housing costs).
Quick next step
- Tell me your geography (e.g., US nationwide, specific metros), time span, and whether you want renter, owner, or both.
I can provision a research environment that pulls ACS + HUD + Zillow/Redfin and compute:
- Rent burden: rent-to-income, 30/50% thresholds.
- Ownership HAI: income vs P&I at given rates, taxes, insurance.
- Supply-side: inventory, months of supply, vacancy proxies.
